question: Can a prepacked tile-local segment reducer become the smaller keyed/segmented producer by slicing D and splitting H64 across lanes before global dKV writes?

implementation.contract: one CTA owns one prepacked (token-block, selected-block, compressed-group) segment and one D block; threads split the H dimension, reduce partials in shared memory, then emit one reduced compressed-dKV D-block row

read: The D-block head-split reducer proves the prepacked segment contract is correct and helps at S512, but it collapses at S4096 because segment-owned CTAs still recompute contribution math and create hundreds of thousands to millions of small CTAs. D64 reduces CTA count but remains far above the sparse-atomic wall. This closes scalar segmented CTA variants as a speed path.

next: Keep the TileLang selected-limit compressed-dKV kernel as the active candidate.
Do not pursue more scalar segment-owned D-block reducer tuning unless it is only for correctness diagnostics.
Next concrete TileLang/CK work should fuse or adjacent-produce P and scaled dP and reuse GPU LI route metadata so the bridge stops paying selected-state/materialized-probability costs.
